jokes.text: It's pronounced "weener-slave."
jokes.setup: Someone mispronounces what seems like a normal name 'Winerslav'
jokes.punchline: The correction makes it sound like an inappropriate sexual term
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure with excellent economy. The correction creates a sexual innuendo in the middle of a sexual harassment seminar, which is perfectly ironic.

jokes.text: That is an example of a very appropriate and very,very funny joke.
jokes.setup: The instructor just made an inappropriate sexual pun about his own name
jokes.punchline: He declares it 'very appropriate' despite being wildly inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good ironic follow-up that shows the instructor's complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: I was just swinging my arm and she walked into me. Twice.
jokes.setup: Jenna denies hitting an Asian page, claims it was accidental
jokes.punchline: The word 'Twice' reveals it happened multiple times, undermining her defense
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna self-incrimination. The escalation word 'Twice' completely undermines her excuse.

jokes.text: We got the life rights? It's not janie jimplin anymore?
jokes.setup: Discussion about a Janis Joplin biopic and naming rights
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous fake name 'janie jimplin' as a rights-cleared alternative
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on the absurdity of rights clearance. Sets up the even better 'Jackie Jormp-Jomp'

jokes.text: It looks like your character's going to be called jackie jormp-jomp.
jokes.setup: After the previous fake name, expectation of another silly alternative
jokes.punchline: An even more ridiculous name that barely resembles the original
jokes.explanation: Instantly iconic nonsense name that became a cultural touchstone. Perfect escalation from the previous fake name.

jokes.text: Oh,okay. So what's the good news?
jokes.setup: Jack delivers terrible news about her movie
jokes.punchline: Jenna's oblivious optimism that there must be good news too
jokes.explanation: Pure Jenna character comedy - her narcissistic inability to process bad news

jokes.text: You're holding that upsiddown.
jokes.setup: Discussion about focus group scores, Jenna seems pleased with '100'
jokes.punchline: The score is actually 001 - complete failure
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection gag. Visual element works well within dialogue structure.

jokes.text: But what about my duet with jimi hendrickson at woodstocks?
jokes.setup: Jenna referencing her fake movie's historical moment
jokes.punchline: Mangled names 'jimi hendrickson' and plural 'woodstocks'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay continuing the theme of mangled historical references

jokes.text: someday there will be a black president
jokes.setup: Jenna performing a song from her fake 1960s biopic
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic political prediction sung as period music
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic humor playing on the absurdity of the fake biopic

jokes.text: Do you need a sex tape released? 'Cause I got a wrdne.
jokes.setup: Jack needs help with PR for Jenna's movie
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately offers to release a sex tape as a solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment - her shamelessness and willingness to do anything for fame

jokes.text: It's night vision and you can see that his buddy is robbing me.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions having a sex tape
jokes.punchline: The bizarre detail that someone is being robbed during the sex tape
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected escalation. The casual mention of being robbed during sex is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: But she knows what she did.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions setting aside her feud with Raven Simone
jokes.punchline: Ominous but vague reference to their conflict
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy playing on celebrity feuds. The vagueness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're on s-e-x probation.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explains why Liz can't be at work
jokes.punchline: He spells out 'sex' like it's a curse word children shouldn't hear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - his childlike prudishness about the word 'sex'

jokes.text: Mr. Donaghy,help! Rule breaking!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to get work materials from Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Kenneth calls for help like Liz is attacking him
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's over-the-top adherence to rules creates good physical comedy.

jokes.text: I learned that from dr. King! I'm brave!
jokes.setup: Kenneth practices passive resistance against giving Liz scripts
jokes.punchline: He compares his minor workplace defiance to MLK's civil rights work
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor from Kenneth's complete lack of perspective on historical importance

jokes.text: Remember that time I came back from the world economic forum with mono
jokes.setup: Jack tries to relate to Liz's work withdrawal
jokes.punchline: His 'relatable' story involves an elite international conference
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack character comedy - trying to relate while revealing his elite lifestyle

jokes.text: Hey,i brighten their day.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions jabbering at doormen as a symptom of work withdrawal
jokes.punchline: Liz defensively claims she brightens doormen's days
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Liz's self-delusion about her social interactions

jokes.text: "constant cving."
jokes.setup: Liz asks what Frank's hat says
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical phrase that sounds like it should mean something
jokes.explanation: Typical 30 Rock nonsense visual gag. Frank's hats are a running element.

jokes.text: He could do better.
jokes.setup: Frank's hat has meaningless text on it
jokes.punchline: Jack treats it like legitimate commentary worthy of critique
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction treating nonsense seriously. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: The whole thing is loosely based on a evening I spent with isiah thomas.
jokes.setup: Discussion about sexual harassment policies
jokes.punchline: Jack casually references a specific inappropriate encounter with a real person
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy revealing Jack's problematic past through casual reference

jokes.text: And said pretty boys like me shouldn't be filling their heads with ideas.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explains why he can't read the harassment manual
jokes.punchline: He calls himself a 'pretty boy' with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth delusion. His complete lack of self-awareness about his appearance.

jokes.text: Hello. Coworker.
jokes.setup: Normal workplace greeting expected
jokes.punchline: Frank's stilted, overly formal greeting to avoid harassment
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy through awkward dialogue. Shows effects of harassment training.

jokes.text: Like you were,in poland.
jokes.setup: Liz compares herself to a doctor helping people
jokes.punchline: She assumes the doorman was a doctor in Poland, revealing her assumptions
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment showing Liz's unconscious classism/assumptions about immigrants

jokes.text: Not even the time I had that virus they kept saying only raccoons get.
jokes.setup: Liz talks about never missing shows despite illness
jokes.punchline: She somehow contracted a disease that only affects raccoons
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. The casualness of mentioning a raccoon disease is perfect.

jokes.text: What? This building has a gym?
jokes.setup: Emily mentions going to the building's fitness center
jokes.punchline: Liz has lived there 4 years but never knew it existed
jokes.explanation: Standard character comedy about Liz being oblivious to her surroundings

jokes.text: Right now,i should be standing in a toilet stall so nobody bothers me while I eat lunch.
jokes.setup: Liz explains what she misses about work
jokes.punchline: She misses hiding in bathroom stalls to eat undisturbed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character comedy. The specificity and sadness of eating alone in bathrooms.

jokes.text: A a decoy candy drawer to throw others off the trail?
jokes.setup: Emily mentions having a secret candy drawer
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately understands advanced candy-hiding strategies
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Liz's expertise in workplace food hoarding strategies

jokes.text: He has a ridge on the section of the skull associated with deviousness.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explains why he doesn't trust Pete
jokes.punchline: He uses phrenology (outdated skull reading) as reasoning
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kenneth character comedy. His belief in outdated pseudoscience is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And not because of my acute ventralitis.
jokes.setup: Kenneth says his heart skips a beat when he sees Daphne
jokes.punchline: He clarifies it's not due to his made-up medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth comedy with his hypochondriac tendencies and made-up medical terms

jokes.text: I actually haven't spoken to her yet, but I have a long-term plan to marry her,
jokes.setup: Kenneth talks about dating a dancer
jokes.punchline: He's never spoken to her but already plans to marry her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth stalker-adjacent behavior delivered with innocent sincerity

jokes.text: But someone atthe today show made me eat an unripe bana in front of her.
jokes.setup: Kenneth reporting possible harassment
jokes.punchline: The 'harassment' is being made to eat an unripe banana
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth innocence comedy - he doesn't understand what constitutes harassment

jokes.text: I will not be able to attend your wedding. Because of a prior conflict.
jokes.setup: Frank making conversation with Kenneth
jokes.punchline: He preemptively declines a wedding invitation that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Excellent Frank deadpan humor - his antisocial nature expressed formally

jokes.text: Weekday.
jokes.setup: Liz asks what the special occasion is for Emily's luxurious outfit
jokes.punchline: Emily's one-word response that it's just a regular weekday
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and understatement. Shows wealth gap through single word.

jokes.text: Short for shabby. Hilarious.
jokes.setup: Emily uses the abbreviation 'shabs'
jokes.punchline: She explains and declares her own wordplay hilarious
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about rich people thinking they're funny

jokes.text: This is,like,your version of sweatpants and pop-tarts? Not too shabs. Short for shabby. Hilarious.
jokes.setup: Liz comparing Emily's luxury to her own casual wear
jokes.punchline: Emily's 'shabs' abbreviation and declaring it hilarious
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Emily's oblivious privilege vs Liz's working-class reality.

jokes.text: Or write a cookbook specifically for mixed-race children.
jokes.setup: Emily suggests ways to get rich
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific and questionable cookbook niche
jokes.explanation: Bizarre specificity makes this memorable. The absurdity is well-executed.

jokes.text: Or have mild lupus and great insurance.
jokes.setup: Continuing suggestions for getting rich
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting profiting from chronic illness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Emily's casual attitude toward exploiting tragedy

jokes.text: Or marry a rich, old dude who dies. Clive,i wanna say?
jokes.setup: Emily continuing money-making suggestions
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions her dead husband with uncertain name
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect character moment. She's unsure of her dead husband's name - very wealthy widow behavior.

jokes.text: Those look great on you. Then I'm buying them.
jokes.setup: Shopping scene with Emily and Liz
jokes.punchline: Emily's immediate purchase decision based on compliment
jokes.explanation: Shows Emily's casual relationship with money. Simple but effective wealth humor.

jokes.text: and it's gone just like that? Just like that. Thank you,dr. Binderman.
jokes.setup: Emily discussing something medical
jokes.punchline: Casual interaction with presumably plastic surgeon
jokes.explanation: Implies casual cosmetic procedures. The throwaway nature makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I forgot to watch my show.
jokes.setup: Liz has been obsessing about missing work all day
jokes.punchline: She forgot to watch the show she was so worried about
jokes.explanation: Good ironic payoff. Perfect economy showing how she got distracted from her obsession.

jokes.text: dora the explorer's backpack! Backpack, is map in you tonight?
jokes.setup: Red carpet interview setting
jokes.punchline: Child reporter asks nonsensical Dora the Explorer question
jokes.explanation: Absurd kids' show reference disrupting adult red carpet coverage

jokes.text: Dotcom,i forgot to get you extra mustard.
jokes.setup: Kenneth distributing lunches normally
jokes.punchline: He confesses to forgetting Dotcom's specific condiment preference
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalating mustard conflict between Kenneth and Dotcom

jokes.text: Except for the mustard that comes with the sandwich.
jokes.setup: Kenneth says Dotcom will never get mustard
jokes.punchline: He clarifies the exception, making his threat meaningless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic - his revenge is immediately undermined by his honesty

jokes.text: Oh,really? I love you too,dotcom.
jokes.setup: Dotcom says he's sorry and didn't mean to hurt Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Kenneth interprets this as a love declaration
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Kenneth's emotional misinterpretation

jokes.text: Yeah,well, liz lemon's in jail now.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions Liz's instructions not to let Tracy start adventures
jokes.punchline: Tracy exaggerates her suspension as being 'in jail'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy exaggeration and self-justification for ignoring rules

jokes.text: Are you daphne? Yeah,you're fired.
jokes.setup: Tracy decides to solve the Kenneth-Dotcom conflict
jokes.punchline: His solution is to immediately fire the woman they're fighting over
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic and economy. His 'solution' is characteristically extreme and misses the point.

jokes.text: And now the cyrus family has decided to rock a bunch ofunky hats.
jokes.setup: Jenna complaining about red carpet problems
jokes.punchline: She's threatened by the Cyrus family's hat choices
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna narcissism - seeing other people's fashion as personal attacks

jokes.text: Did jackie jormp-jomp give up when those vampires attacked woodstocks?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to motivate Jenna to continue promoting
jokes.punchline: Reference to more ridiculous fake movie mythology
jokes.explanation: Good callback building the absurd mythology of the fake movie

jokes.text: Was that me? Did they just say I'm dead?
jokes.setup: Memorial segment on TV mentions various entertainment figures
jokes.punchline: Jenna realizes she's been included in the 'In Memoriam' segment
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the 'fake death' plot. Jenna's reaction is appropriately confused.

jokes.text: Maroney. Picture. Dead. Got it.
jokes.setup: Connor hearing about the situation
jokes.punchline: His robotic understanding of key points
jokes.explanation: Good supporting character moment. Connor's efficient processing is funny contrast to the chaos.

jokes.text: You being dead is the best thing that ever happened to this movie.
jokes.setup: Jenna thinks being declared dead is a disaster
jokes.punchline: Jack sees it as a marketing opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack ruthlessness - turning tragedy into profit opportunity

jokes.text: Jenna. I wanna tupac you.
jokes.setup: Jack explains his marketing plan
jokes.punchline: Using Tupac's posthumous success as a verb for marketing strategy
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay turning a proper noun into a verb. Very quotable and clever.

jokes.text: Or rusty,the bear fromthe magicals.
jokes.setup: Jack gives examples of posthumous success like Janis Joplin
jokes.punchline: He includes a fictional bear from a made-up show
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor mixing real and fake cultural references

jokes.text: I watched my whole church group get eaten by a bear.
jokes.setup: Jenna says she can play dead
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals traumatic backstory as qualification
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor. Jenna's casual delivery of horrific backstory is perfect.

jokes.text: Why don't you drop those dockers and gimme a piece of that sweet ass?
jokes.setup: Instructor asks if Liz has any questions after harassment training
jokes.punchline: She immediately sexually harasses him in response
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Liz has learned nothing. The ironic reversal is well-executed.

jokes.text: Did i? Or did he put his mouth on my fingers?
jokes.setup: Jack confronts Liz about putting fingers in instructor's mouth
jokes.punchline: Liz's twisted reframing of the incident
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Liz's defensive rationalization

jokes.text: I'm on humanity leave.
jokes.setup: Liz explains why she needs more time off
jokes.punchline: She coins a pretentious term for her vacation
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay creating a pseudo-sophisticated term for being lazy

jokes.text: Syllabus of usoff vagabonds,martyrs,and quilts.
jokes.setup: Jack asks what Indigo Girls song that's from
jokes.punchline: Fake song title that perfectly parodies their style
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of Indigo Girls' earnest, wordy song titles. Very specific cultural humor.

jokes.text: Like that hbo show. John adams.
jokes.setup: Tracy talks about friendship and trust in entourage
jokes.punchline: Confuses 'Entourage' HBO show with historical miniseries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy confusion mixing contemporary and historical references

jokes.text: Heavy is the head that eats the crayons.
jokes.setup: Tracy reflecting on leadership responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'heavy is the head that wears the crown'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy malapropism. The crayon reference fits his childlike character perfectly.

jokes.text: I don't feel anything.
jokes.setup: Jack tells Pete that Jenna is dead
jokes.punchline: Pete's completely emotionless response reveals his true feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Economy is excellent - four words capture Pete's relationship with Jenna.

jokes.text: Bm-ing like a rock star?
jokes.setup: Liz lists the benefits of her new healthy lifestyle
jokes.punchline: She euphemistically brags about her bowel movements
jokes.explanation: Typical Liz oversharing about bodily functions. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Just think,an hour ago they were brawling in a parking lot, But tonight, they're gonna be stars.
jokes.setup: Tracy introduces replacement dancers
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally hired people who were fighting in a parking lot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - his quick solution creates obvious new problems

jokes.text: No,i used to be a neurosurgeon before jasmine guy and I wrote our cookbook,
jokes.setup: Emily explaining why the brain needs stimulation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of co-writing a cookbook with random 90s actress
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback tying together random earlier joke with specific celebrity reference

jokes.text: Punch me in the face,liz.
jokes.setup: Emily explaining their 'special activity' to combat brain atrophy
jokes.punchline: The activity is literally punching each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. The reveal that rich housewives have a fight club is absurd.

jokes.text: I brought a roll of quarters to hold in my fist.
jokes.setup: Emily wants Liz to punch her
jokes.punchline: She came prepared with an improvised weapon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the wealthy women take their fight club seriously

jokes.text: The coast guard won't officially declare her dead yet, but what the pirates did to that boat!
jokes.setup: TV tribute to supposedly dead Jenna
jokes.punchline: Elaborately fake death story involving pirates
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the fake death story

jokes.text: Especially the woman on the right.
jokes.setup: Host comments on tribute to Jenna and another woman
jokes.punchline: Implies the other woman looks like she should have died first
jokes.explanation: Dark visual humor about appearances and mortality expectations

jokes.text: It has the year I was born on it. Therealyear, not the actress year.
jokes.setup: Jenna sees her obituary graphic on TV
jokes.punchline: She's more upset about her real age being revealed than being declared dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment. Her vanity trumps even death concerns.

jokes.text: Whole grain,low fat I know you want a piece of that
jokes.setup: Memorial tribute to Jenna with her 'music'
jokes.punchline: The terrible dancers sing about healthy food as a memorial
jokes.explanation: Absurd musical tribute with inappropriate dancers. Visual comedy element.

jokes.text: still alive.Not yet 32. Sorry,jack. Worth it.
jokes.setup: Jenna decides to reveal she's alive on live TV
jokes.punchline: She corrects her age even while breaking character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna priorities - she ruins the marketing plan just to correct her age

jokes.text: You guys mess up everything you touch, and then I have to come in here and fix it.
jokes.setup: Liz returns to work and sees the chaos
jokes.punchline: She immediately falls back into her enabling pattern while claiming to be mad
jokes.explanation: Good character payoff showing Liz can't help but return to her codependent role

jokes.text: Why are you smiling? You're freaking me out.
jokes.setup: Liz yells at them while visibly happy
jokes.punchline: They notice the contradiction between her words and expression
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy beat showing Liz is happy to be back despite her complaints

jokes.text: You're crazy!
jokes.setup: Liz gives them an aggressive group hug
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's panicked reaction to unexpected physical affection
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy showing Kenneth's discomfort with physical contact
